title: Diplodium bryophilum
taxonomy: Diplodium bryophilum is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This species is classified as a terrestrial orchid, which typically grows in the ground rather than on other plants. Diplodium bryophilum is native to Australia, where it can be found in various habitats including forests and grasslands. Further classification of this orchid species is as follows: it is a member of the genus Diplodium.
uses: Diplodium bryophilum is known for its medicinal properties in traditional Australian Aboriginal medicine. However, specific information about its uses is limited.
